figures.caption: Figure 1: Exact solutions for the benchmark problems. The horizontal line in (a) at 𝑦 = 0.5 represents the solution for the 1D Poisson problem.

Figure 3: Accuracies of the error estimates across different discretizations, measured as ‖𝑒true − 𝑒method‖2 on respective grid points, for all benchmark problems. Averaged over 10 runs, bands show one standard deviation.

Figure 2: Error estimates for a well-trained PINN on the heat equation, (a)–(d) at four different time-points across space and (e) 𝐿2 error over time, including the bound 𝑒bound. The FDM-based methods use a 64× 64 spatio-temporal grid (time slices shown at nearest grid point to specified values); 𝑒bound uses 64 spatial points with adaptive time integration (up to 165 steps at 𝑡 = 1).
